articles.title: Emergency services respond to incident in Middleton town centre
articles.excerpt: Emergency services attended an incident in Middleton town centre involving a man on a roof, leading to a significant police presence in the area.
articles.content_html:
Emergency services responded to an incident in Middleton town centre on Tuesday afternoon, involving a man on a roof. Police and other emergency services arrived at the scene, creating a significant presence in the area.

Witnesses reported seeing emergency vehicles and personnel surrounding the location as efforts were made to resolve the situation. The nature of the incident has not been disclosed, and no injuries have been reported.

Authorities have cordoned off the area while they manage the situation. Local residents and businesses may experience disruptions as a result of the ongoing response.

Further updates will be provided as more information becomes available.
articles.image_credit: Rept0n1x / CC BY-SA 2.0 via Wikimedia Commons
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Middleton McDonald's Reopens Following Major Redesign
articles.excerpt: McDonald's in Middleton has reopened after a significant refurbishment aimed at enhancing customer and staff experiences, featuring updated technology and improved facilities.
articles.content_html:
A McDonald's restaurant in Middleton has reopened following a major refurbishment designed to enhance the experience for both customers and staff. The redesign includes a new layout, updated technology, and separate facilities for delivery couriers.

The restaurant, owned by local franchisee Gerald Thompson, has undergone changes as part of McDonald's latest reimagining programme. This initiative aims to improve speed, efficiency, and choice for customers. The refurbishment features a redesigned kitchen and dining area that supports digital ordering through the McDonald's app, allowing customers to order in advance and either collect their food or have it delivered to their table upon arrival.

The front counter has been restructured to create dedicated areas for different ordering methods, which McDonald's states will reduce congestion and shorten queues around self-service ordering screens. Additionally, improvements have been made behind the scenes, including a larger order assembly area and dedicated preparation spaces for dine-in meals.

Gerald Thompson, who operates 18 McDonald's restaurants across Greater Manchester, expressed pride in reinvesting in the Middleton location, which he first opened as a franchisee in 2000. He noted that the redesign provides a fresh look and enhances the experience for both customers and the restaurant's team of over eighty staff members.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Man found guilty of murdering teenager over stolen e-bike in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King, 60, has been found guilty of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who stole an e-bike from another boy in Middleton last year.
articles.content_html:
A man has been found guilty of murdering a teenager who stole an e-bike. Terrence King, 60, killed 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines by deliberately driving his car into him in Middleton, Greater Manchester, on 22 July 2025.

The jury heard that Simmonds-Caines had stolen the £3,000 bike from another boy on the Langley Estate. Following a fracas, he rode off on the e-bike, prompting King to pursue him in his vehicle.

King admitted to manslaughter but denied murder. However, after a two-week trial, he was convicted of murder and is scheduled to be sentenced on 23 July at Minshull Street Crown Court in Manchester.

The courtroom erupted with screams of joy from the victim's family and friends as the verdict was announced, with shouts of "thank you" heard from those present. CCTV footage captured the moments leading up to the incident, showing the events that unfolded before Simmonds-Caines was struck.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Crash Closes Heywood Old Road in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Crews are responding to a crash on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, which has resulted in the road being closed. Drivers are advised to avoid the area.
articles.content_html:
A crash has occurred on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, prompting crews to respond to the incident. The road is currently closed as emergency services manage the situation.

Drivers are advised to avoid the area while the response is underway. No further details about the crash have been released.

Authorities have not provided information regarding any injuries or the cause of the crash. Updates will follow as more information becomes available.

Motorists should seek alternative routes to prevent delays during the closure.

articles.title: Community protest planned in Middleton town centre next month
articles.excerpt: Organisers have announced plans for a public protest in Middleton on 8 August, inviting residents to attend and share their views on issues including illegal immigration and community concerns.
articles.summary: A demonstration has been announced for Middleton Gardens on Saturday 8 August. Organisers say the event will feature guest speakers and provide an opportunity for people to express their views on immigration and related issues.
articles.content_html:
A public protest has been scheduled to take place in Middleton town centre next month.

Organisers have announced that the event will begin at 1pm on Saturday 8 August at Middleton Gardens and are encouraging residents who wish to attend to do so peacefully and respectfully.

The demonstration is being promoted as a protest against illegal immigration and is expected to include speeches from invited guests and community campaigners.

Event details announced

According to promotional material shared online, the gathering will take place at Middleton Gardens, with attendees invited to bring flags and take part in the demonstration.

Named speakers currently include Ashlea Simon, Jamie Thomason and PagUK Billy, with organisers indicating that additional speakers may be announced closer to the event.

The event is being organised by campaigner Jamie Thomason, who has invited members of the public wishing to speak to contact him directly.

Community response expected

Public demonstrations often attract a range of opinions and viewpoints from residents. Organisers say the event is intended to provide a platform for people to voice concerns and discuss issues they believe affect local communities.

As with all public gatherings, attendees are expected to comply with relevant laws and any conditions imposed by local authorities or police to ensure the safety of participants and the wider public.

Residents advised to plan ahead

People visiting Middleton town centre on the day of the protest are advised to be aware of the planned gathering, which may lead to increased footfall in and around Middleton Gardens during the afternoon.

This report is based on promotional material and public social media posts advertising the event. Rochdale Daily is reporting the planned gathering as a matter of public interest and does not endorse any political organisation, campaign group or viewpoint associated with the event.
articles.image_credit: Rept0n1x / CC BY-SA 2.0 via Wikimedia Commons
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports the existence of a planned public demonstration. The views expressed by organisers, speakers or attendees are their own. Attendance at any public event should be peaceful and in accordance with applicable laws and public-order requirements.
articles.right_to_reply: Event organisers, local residents, Greater Manchester Police and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Middleton councillors explore new measures to improve town-centre safety
articles.excerpt: Reform UK councillors in Middleton are considering a cycling dismount zone, a review of CCTV provision and wider community-safety measures aimed at making the town centre safer and more welcoming.
articles.summary: Councillors say they are gathering advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before deciding whether new restrictions or improvements should be introduced in Middleton town centre.
articles.content_html:
Middleton councillors are exploring a range of measures designed to improve pedestrian safety and make the town centre a more welcoming place for residents, visitors and businesses.

Reform UK councillors elected in May said they have been examining whether a cycling dismount zone could be introduced in parts of Middleton town centre.

The proposal is intended to address concerns about anti-social behaviour involving bicycles while improving safety for people walking through busy areas.

Focus on practical and enforceable measures

Councillors said an important part of the work will be establishing whether any new restriction could be enforced effectively.

They are seeking advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before any decision is made.

The group said it wants to ensure that any action introduced is practical, proportionate and capable of making a visible difference.

Review of CCTV provision

The councillors are also calling for a review of the town centre’s CCTV network.

They said residents should be able to understand how public-safety infrastructure is being used and whether it is helping to deter anti-social behaviour, support enforcement and improve confidence in the town centre.

The review could consider how cameras are monitored, how footage is used and whether the current network is delivering the intended outcomes.

Creating a welcoming town centre

The statement highlights concerns involving bicycles, e-bikes and e-scooters, while stressing the wider goal of creating a safe and positive environment for everyone.

Councillors said Middleton town centre should be a place where people feel comfortable spending time, shopping and supporting local businesses.

They are continuing to gather information before bringing forward any formal proposals.

Residents invited to contribute

Residents, businesses, pedestrians and cyclists are being invited to share constructive feedback as the work develops.

The councillors said local experiences will be important in shaping measures that balance pedestrian safety, accessibility and the needs of people travelling through the town centre.

This report is based on a public statement issued by Reform UK councillors in Rochdale borough regarding anti-social behaviour and safety in Middleton town centre. No final policy decision has yet been made.
articles.image_credit: Rept0n1x / CC BY-SA 2.0 via Wikimedia Commons
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports proposals and views expressed by elected councillors. No cycling dismount zone, CCTV change or other new restriction has yet been formally approved or introduced.
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, Greater Manchester Police, Middleton residents, local businesses, cyclists and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.
